up.method: MIMS Vmax, accelerated from 0.66 to 4.32 mmol/gDW/h (11→72 μmol/min/gDW); Km(app)=0.04-0.07 μM

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: these chemostats were maintained under stable dilution rates of D = 0.0057 h−1 for “M. acidophilus” 29... the H2 oxidation rates of “M. acidophilus” 29 during MIMS incubations accelerated over time... reaching rates comparable to those foun
growth_records.notes: CH4-limited chemostat D=0.0057 h−1; μ=D; td=ln2/0.0057=121.6 h. Table 1: Vmax CH4=75 μmol/min/gDW ->4.50 mmol/gDW/h; Vmax H2 11->72 μmol/min/gDW ->0.66->4.32 mmol/gDW/h; Km CH4=2.5-4 μM, Km H2=0.04-0.07 μM. CAVEAT lowering confidence: chemostat culture contained a Burkholderia contaminant possessing a Group 3d [NiFe] hydrogenase; though M. acidophilus 29 was 4-fold more abundant by metagenomic read coverage, the H2 oxidation activity cannot be attributed solely to strain 29. No dry weight measured; DW assumed = mean of other strains (250.3 mg DW/L). No yield reported.
